{{神社| 名称=鳥越八幡神社| 位置=| 所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224| 創建=[[江戸時代]]| 社格=| 祭神=[[応神天皇]]| 本殿=| 例祭=| 画像=}}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある神社である。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== この神社は、社伝によれば初代[[新庄藩]]主[[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]][[戸沢定盛]]によって創建されたとされる。
==文化財== *国指定重要文化財 **鳥越八幡神社本殿・拝殿
==所在地== *山形県新庄市鳥越1224
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/top.html 鳥越八幡宮サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりこえはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:新庄市]] [[Category:山形県の神社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=鳥越八幡神社| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|scale:25000}}| 所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224| 創建=伝 [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）| 社格=| 祭神=[[応神天皇]]| 本殿=| 例祭=[[8月15日]]| 画像=}}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある神社である。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== この神社は、社伝によれば初代[[新庄藩]]主[[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]][[戸沢定盛]]によって創建されたとされる。戸沢定盛が、新庄市の北部にある荒小屋地区で鷹狩りを行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しみ、荒小屋八幡宮で鷹が戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったという。定盛は、八幡[[菩薩]]の神威に感じ入り、荒小屋八幡宮を鳥越の地に移し、現在も残る社殿を建立したのが由来である。
鳥越の地は[[羽州街道]]に沿っており、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する。[[源義経]]の東下りのルートに当たり、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所である。鳥越八幡神社は、鳥越楯の跡地に建てられた。
なお、鳥越八幡神社の元となった荒小屋八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな祠として存在している。
==文化財== *国指定[[重要文化財]] **鳥越八幡神社本殿・拝殿
==境内== *[[鳥居]] *本殿･･･創建時(1638年)より現存する[[新庄市]]では最古の建造物 *拝殿･･･[[1641年]]建立 *七所明神社･･･応神天皇の第2皇子である大山守命の左手が祀られている。
==所在地== *山形県新庄市鳥越1224
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりこえはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:新庄市]] [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] {{shinto-stub}}

なお、鳥越八幡神社の元となった荒小屋八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな祠として存在している。
==文化財== *国指定[[重要文化財]] **鳥越八幡神社本殿・拝殿
==境内== *[[鳥居]] *本殿･･･創建時(1638年)より現存する[[新庄市]]では最古の建造物 *拝殿･･･[[1641年]]建立 *七所明神社･･･応神天皇の第2皇子である大山守命の左手が祀られている。

{{神社| 名称=鳥越八幡神社| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|scale:25000}}| 所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224| 創建=伝 [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）| 社格=| 祭神=[[応神天皇]]| 本殿=| 例祭=[[8月15日]]| 画像=}}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある神社である。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== この神社は、社伝によれば初代[[新庄藩]]主[[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]][[戸沢定盛]]によって創建されたとされる。戸沢定盛が、新庄市の北部にある荒小屋地区で鷹狩りを行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しみ、荒小屋八幡宮で鷹が戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったという。定盛は、八幡大[[菩薩]]の神威に感じ入り、荒小屋八幡宮を鳥越の地に移し、現在も残る社殿を建立したのが由来である。
鳥越の地は[[羽州街道]]に沿っており、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する。[[源義経]]の東下りのルートに当たり、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所である。鳥越八幡神社は、鳥越楯の跡地に建てられた。
なお、鳥越八幡神社の元となった荒小屋八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな祠として存在している。
==文化財== ;[[重要文化財]]（国指定） *八幡神社本殿 *八幡神社拝殿
==境内== *[[鳥居]] *本殿 - 一間社流造。創建時（寛永15年・1638年）より現存する[[新庄市]]では最古の建造物。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造。元禄4年（[[1641年]]）建立 *七所[[明神]]社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である大山守命の左手が祀られている。
==所在地== *山形県新庄市鳥越1224
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

revision.comment: やや加筆
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=鳥越八幡神社 |画像= |所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224 ||位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|scale:25000}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]） |本殿=一間社流造銅板葺 |例祭=[[8月15日]] |神事=鳥越神楽（8月15日） }}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある[[神社]]（[[郷社|旧郷社]]）である。社殿が[[重要文化財|国の重要文化財]]に指定されている。
==社名== 正式名称は「'''八幡神社'''」。「'''鳥越八幡宮'''」とも称す。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠|正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[平入]]の[[入母屋造]]銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。
==文化財== ===国指定=== *本殿（重要文化財） *拝殿（同上） ===新庄市指定=== *鳥越神楽（無形民俗文化財）
==脚注== {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、昭和38年
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりこえはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:山形県の重要文化財]] [[Category:新庄市]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}

{{神社 |名称=鳥越八幡神社 |画像= |所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224 ||位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|scale:25000}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]） |本殿=一間社流造銅板葺 |例祭=[[8月15日]] |神事=鳥越神楽（8月15日） }}

==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、昭和38年
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。 *青麻神社
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、1963年
== 関連項目 == *[[松田甚次郎]] - 昭和初期の地元の農村指導者。境内で上演した演劇用の土舞台が現存する。
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

==社名== 正式名称は「'''八幡神社'''」。「'''鳥越八幡宮'''」とも称す。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。 *青麻神社
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、1963年
== 関連項目 == *[[松田甚次郎]] - 昭和初期の地元の農村指導者。境内で上演した演劇用の土舞台が現存する。
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

{{神社 |名称=八幡神社 |画像= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224 |位置={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]） |本殿=一間社流造銅板葺 |例祭=[[8月15日]] |神事=鳥越神楽（8月15日） |別名=鳥越八幡神社、鳥越八幡宮 }}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。社殿が[[重要文化財|国の重要文化財]]に指定されている。
==社名== 正式名称は「'''八幡神社'''」。「'''鳥越八幡宮'''」とも称す。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。 *青麻神社
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、1963年
== 関連項目 == *[[松田甚次郎]] - 昭和初期の地元の農村指導者。境内で上演した演劇用の土舞台が現存する。
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}} '''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[annual festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
{{神社 |名称=Yahata Shrine |画像= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |位置={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |祭神=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |社格=Former Gōsha |創建=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |本殿=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |例祭=[[August 15]] |神事=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |別名=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine Name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== Festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Main Hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}} '''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[annual festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
{{神社 |名称=Yahata Shrine |画像= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |位置={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |祭神=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |社格=Former Gōsha |創建=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |本殿=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |例祭=[[August 15]] |神事=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |別名=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine Name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== Festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Main Hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Main Hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]

==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine]]
{{Shinto shrine}}
{{coord missing|Yamagata Prefecture}}

{{Shinto-stub}}

Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== Festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.

The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. {{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}} '''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[annual festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
{{神社 |名称=Yahata Shrine |画像= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |位置={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |祭神=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |社格=Former Gōsha |創建=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |本殿=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |例祭=[[August 15]] |神事=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |別名=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |地図={{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Main Hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * Main Hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]

It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[annual festival]] is on August 15.

{{神社 |name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |address=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |創建=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |本殿=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |reisai=[[August 15]] |神事=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |別名=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |map={{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}} '''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[annual festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |address=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=[[August 15]] |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |map={{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The annual festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]

{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}} '''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=[[August 15]] |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu |map={{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=Torigoe Yahata Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Torikoe Hachimangu}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shinjo Domain]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shinjo, Yamagata]] [[Category:History of Shinjo, Yamagata]]
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine]]
{{Shinto shrine}}
{{coord missing|Yamagata Prefecture}}

The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura

== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.

{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan}}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Torikoe Hachimangu}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Shinjo Domain]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shinjo, Yamagata]] [[Category:History of Shinjo, Yamagata]]
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine]]
{{Shinto shrine}}
{{coord missing|Yamagata Prefecture}}

[[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]] [[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:鳥越八幡神社]] [[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan}}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963
== See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]

When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]] |rank=Former Gōsha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan}}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[main hall]] - Built in [[Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]

==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine]]

The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[Showa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine

22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鳥越八幡神社 up to rev 76348723
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=八幡神社 |画像= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|鳥居と拝殿]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地=[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越1224 |位置={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=鳥越八幡神社}} |祭神=[[応神天皇]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=<sub>伝</sub>[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]） |本殿=一間社流造銅板葺 |例祭=[[8月15日]] |神事=鳥越神楽（8月15日） |別名=鳥越八幡神社、鳥越八幡宮 |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=44|lat_sec=30|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=59|label=鳥越八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''鳥越八幡神社'''（とりごえはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[新庄市]]鳥越にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。社殿が[[重要文化財|国の重要文化財]]に指定されている。
==社名== 正式名称は「'''八幡神社'''」。「'''鳥越八幡宮'''」とも称す。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。 *青麻神社
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、1963年
== 関連項目 == *[[松田甚次郎]] - 昭和初期の地元の農村指導者。境内で上演した演劇用の土舞台が現存する。
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

==社名== 正式名称は「'''八幡神社'''」。「'''鳥越八幡宮'''」とも称す。
==祭神== *[[応神天皇]]
==歴史== 社伝によれば、[[寛喜]]元年（[[1229年]]）に鳥越義宣が[[相模国]]から[[鶴岡八幡宮]]を[[勧請]]、これを「新八幡宮」と称したことに始まるといい<ref>『神社名鑑』。</ref>、当初は荒小屋（現新庄市北部）に祀られていたが、初代[[新庄藩|新庄藩主]][[戸沢政盛]]の[[養子]]となった[[戸沢定盛]]が荒小屋で[[鷹狩り]]を行った際、愛[[鷹]]が行方不明になったのを悲しんで八幡宮に戻ってくるよう祈ったところ、鷹が定盛の元に舞い戻ったため、その神威に感じ入った定盛が[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）に鳥越の地の[[鳥越楯跡]]に社殿を建立して八幡宮を移し、以降代々の藩主に崇敬されたという。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社に列した。
なお、鳥越は[[羽州街道]]に沿った、[[新庄城]]下に入る手前に位置する地で、[[源義経]]の東下りの経路にも当たっており、[[中世]]には[[土豪]]により「鳥越楯」が築かれた場所でもあった。また、元宮である荒小屋の八幡宮は、現在も荒小屋地区に小さな[[祠]]として存在している。
==祭祀== {{節stub}} 例祭に'''鳥越神楽'''（市指定[[無形民俗文化財]]）が奉納される。
==社殿== *[[本殿]] - 寛永15年（1638年）の建立になる現存する新庄市最古の建造物。大型の[[流造|一間社流造]]で、屋根は[[茅葺]]であったが、近年[[銅板葺]]に改められた。もとは内外とも[[丹]]や黒[[漆]]で彩色されていたようで、各部にその跡が残っている。意匠として、[[妻]]に太い[[虹梁|虹梁（こうりょう）]]を渡して[[大瓶束|大瓶束（たいへいづか）]]の[[結綿 (建築)|結綿（ゆいわた）]]に鬼面の彫刻を施し、また虹梁を支える[[力士]]像が彫刻されている。 *[[拝殿]] - 新庄藩2代藩主[[戸沢正誠]]による[[元禄]]4年（[[1691年]]）の建立。桁行3間梁間2間、[[入母屋造]][[平入]]、銅板葺で、正面に1間の[[向拝|向拝（ごはい）]]を有し、背面からは[[幣殿]]を突出させて本殿に接続している。 本殿が各部の彫刻や色彩に富んだ[[江戸時代]]初期の華やかな意匠を有するのに対し、拝殿は総[[素木造|素木（しらき）造]]で江戸時代中期の堅実な特色を示し、ともに当地方における当時の建築技術の粋を伝える建造物として、[[昭和]]61年（[[1986年]]）12月20日に国の重要文化財に指定された<ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html 新庄市ホームページ]（平成21年8月1日閲覧）。</ref>。
他に[[鳥居]]などがある。
==境内社== *七所明神社 - 応神天皇の第2皇子である[[大山守命]]の左手が祀られている。 *青麻神社
==文化財== ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 *拝殿 ===新庄市指定無形民俗文化財=== *鳥越神楽
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==参考文献== *神社本庁調査部編『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、1963年
== 関連項目 == *[[松田甚次郎]] - 昭和初期の地元の農村指導者。境内で上演した演劇用の土舞台が現存する。
==外部リンク== *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ 鳥越八幡宮サイト]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11675142]] Wikidata name: Torigoe Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= [[File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG|250px|Torii and worship hall]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |location=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{coord|38|44|30|N|140|18|59|E|region:JP-06_scale:25000_region:JP|display=inline,title|name=Torigoe Yahata Shrine}} |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan}}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
