'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).

In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.

The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.

In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.

When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura

'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG |location=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{Coord|38.741667|N|140.316389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan|prefecture={{ill|Shinjo|WD=Q840603}} }}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG |location=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{Coord|38.741667|N|140.316389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan|prefecture={{ill|Shinjo|WD=Q840603}} }}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG |location=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{Coord|38.741667|N|140.316389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan|prefecture={{ill|Shinjo|WD=Q840603}} }}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Shinjō, Yamagata|Shinjō, Yamagata]] Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its [[:en:Festival|Festival]] is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanki|Kanki]] 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]], calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takamori]], the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of [[:en:Tōdō Takatora|Tōdō Takatora]], he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the [[:en:Ushū Kaidō|Ushū Kaidō]] highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.

== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanki|en|Kanki|WD=Q23857088|lt=Kanki}} 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. When {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takamori}}, the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takatora}}, he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|en|Ushū Kaidō|WD=Q3552562|lt=Ushū Kaidō}} highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}

It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=Festival}} is on August 15.

Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:鳥越八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:鳥越八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Torigoe Hachiman Shrine]][[ja:鳥越八幡神社]]
{{Short description|Shrine in Shinjo, Yagamata Prefecture, Japan}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2023}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG |location={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}, [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{Coord|38.741667|N|140.316389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> {{ill|Kanki|en|Kanki|WD=Q23857088|lt=Kanki}} 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan|prefecture={{ill|Shinjo|WD=Q840603}} }}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Shinjō, Yamagata|en|Shinjō, Yamagata|WD=Q840603|lt=Shinjō, Yamagata}} Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=Festival}} is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanki|en|Kanki|WD=Q23857088|lt=Kanki}} 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}, calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takamori}}, the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takatora}}, he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|en|Ushū Kaidō|WD=Q3552562|lt=Ushū Kaidō}} highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku}} 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] *[[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]]

'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It is classified as a former [[Gōsha]]. The shrine buildings are designated as Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government.
== Shrine name == The official name is "'''Yahata Shrine'''". It is also known as "'''Torigoe Hachimangu'''".
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanki|en|Kanki|WD=Q23857088|lt=Kanki|1=Kanki}} 1 (1229) when Torigoe Yoshinobu invited the spirit of Hachiman from [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}, calling it "Shin Hachimangu" <ref>'Shinto Shrine Directory'</ref>. Initially, it was located in Aragoya (now the northern part of Shinjo City). When {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takamori|1=Tōdō Takatora}}, the first lord of [[:en:Shinjo Domain|Shinjo Domain]], became the adopted son of {{ill|Tōdō Takatora|en|Tōdō Takatora|WD=Q1377984|lt=Tōdō Takatora|1=Tōdō Takatora}}, he moved the shrine to Torigoe's Tategawa site in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638) after praying to Hachiman for the return of his lost falcon, which subsequently returned to him.
The shrine was designated a [[Gōsha]] in [[Meiji]] 6 (1873).
Torigoe is located along the {{ill|Ushū Kaidō|en|Ushū Kaidō|WD=Q3552562|lt=Ushū Kaidō|1=Ushū Kaidō}} highway, just before entering the Shinjo Castle town, and was also on the route taken by [[Minamoto no Yoshitsune]] during his eastern journey. In the medieval period, local lords built fortifications known as "Torigoe Tate" in the area. The original shrine in Aragoya still exists today as a small shrine.
== festivals == The festival, held on August 15, features the '''Torigoe Kagura''', which is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Shinjo City.
== Shrine Buildings == * [[Honden|Main hall]] - Built in {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 15 (1638), it is the oldest surviving structure in Shinjo City. The large Nagare-zukuri style hall originally had a thatched roof, which was recently replaced with a copper roof. The hall was originally painted in red and black lacquer, remnants of which remain. The hall features decorative elements such as large cross beams with carved demon masks and supporting statues of warriors. * [[Worship Hall]] - Built in {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|WD=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 4 (1691) by Tōdō Takatora, the second lord of Shinjo Domain. The hall is 3 bays wide and 2 bays deep, with an irimoya roof, copper roofing, and a 1-bay protruding entrance. It connects to the main hall via a Heiden (offering hall). The main hall, with its elaborate early Edo period decorations, and the worship hall, built in the plainer middle Edo period style, both showcase the architectural skills of the time. They were designated Important Cultural Properties in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 61 (1986) <ref>[http://www.city.shinjo.yamagata.jp/2563.html Shinjo City Website] (accessed August 1, 2009).</ref>.
Other structures include the [[Torii]] gate.
== Subordinate Shrines == * Shichisho Shrine - Enshrines the left hand of [[Ōyamamori no Mikoto]], the second prince of Emperor Ōjin. * Aoma Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * main hall * Worship Hall
=== Shinjo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Torigoe Kagura
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Shinto Shrine Research Department, 'Shinto Shrine Directory', [[Shinto Shrine Research Department]], 1963 == See Also == * [[Jinjitsu Matsudaira]] - A local agricultural leader in the early Showa period. A stage for performances remains on the shrine grounds.
== External Links == *[http://torigoehachiman.org/ Torigoe Hachimangu Site]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Yahata Shrine |image= File:TorigoeHachimanJinja Shinjo.JPG |location={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, [[Shinjo City]], Torigoe 1224 |coordinates={{Coord|38.741667|N|140.316389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Rank=Former Gō-sha |established=<sub>Trad.</sub> {{ill|Kanki|en|Kanki|WD=Q23857088|lt=Kanki|1=Kanki}} 1 (1229) |main hall=Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri, Copper Roof |festival=Torigoe Kagura (August 15) |alternative name=Torigoe Hachiman Shrine, Torigoe Hachimangu | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan|prefecture={{ill|Shinjo|WD=Q840603|1=Shinjō, Yamagata}} }}
'''Torigoe Hachiman Shrine''' (鳥越八幡神社, ''Torigoe Hachiman jinja'', also called 鳥越八幡宮, ''Torigoe Hachimangū'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Shinjō, Yamagata|en|Shinjō, Yamagata|WD=Q840603|lt=Shinjō, Yamagata|1=Shinjō, Yamagata}} Prefecture, Japan. It is a [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], dedicated to the [[kami]] [[Hachiman]]. The shrine was founded in 1229, and its {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|WD=Q132241|lt=Festival|1=Festival}} is on August 15.
==Overview==
'''Torigoe Yahata Shrine''' (とりごえはちまんじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Torigoe, [[Shinjo City]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}.
